title: Hexagonal Grid Battle Board with Iron Command Visual Overhaul

requirements.text: Replace the current square grid cells in BattleBoard.tsx and GridTile.tsx with regular hexagons (pointy-top or flat-top orientation). Implement a proper hex grid layout for the 7×4 (player) and 7×4 (enemy) sections using offset or axial coordinates. Hexagon tiles should use CSS clip-path or SVG polygon to render the correct six-sided shape. Unit sprites, HP bars, and drag-and-drop logic must be adapted to work on the hexagonal grid. Gaps between hex tiles should use standard hex grid spacing so tiles tessellate correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: All battle board grid cells are visually rendered as regular hexagons, not squares or rectangles.
Hexagons tessellate correctly with no overlapping or misaligned tiles.
Player and enemy board sections each display a 7×4 hex grid.
Drag-and-drop unit placement works correctly on the hex grid.
Units, HP bars, star indicators, and portraits are correctly centered within their hexagonal cells.
No regression in combat animations or battle phase logic.

requirements.text: Overhaul the overall visual presentation of the game inspired by the Iron Command (钢铁命令) real-time strategy aesthetic: a gritty, high-contrast war-room look with sharp panel edges, tactical overlay styling, and a darker base palette (deep desert night tones — dark sand, aged bronze, deep lapis, ember gold) contrasted by bright unit highlight colors. The battle board should feel like a tactical command map: add a subtle grid coordinate label system (column letters, row numbers) along the board edges, a dark vignette border around the board, and unit tokens that look like military unit markers with bold iconography. The HUD should be redesigned to resemble a tactical command interface with segmented stat blocks (HP, Gold, Round, Streak) displayed in sharp bordered panels. Shop cards should adopt a compact military-briefing card style. All panels (SynergyPanel, Shop, Bench, HUD) should use dark backgrounds with gold and bronze accent borders. Typography for labels and stats should shift to a monospaced or condensed sans-serif for stat numbers while headings retain the Egyptian serif font.

quotes: 主体可以参考钢铁命令对画面进行优化

acceptanceCriteria: The battle board uses a dark tactical map aesthetic with visible coordinate labels along its edges.
A dark vignette or border frame surrounds the battle board.
Unit tokens on the hex board visually resemble military unit markers with bold portrait icons.
The HUD displays HP, Gold, Round, Wins, Losses in sharp segmented bordered panels.
Shop cards use a compact, military-briefing card style with dark background and gold/bronze accents.
SynergyPanel, Bench, and all game panels use dark backgrounds with gold/bronze accent borders.
Stat numbers use a monospaced or condensed sans-serif font; headings retain the Egyptian serif (Cinzel).
Overall color palette is darker (deep desert night tones) with high-contrast accent highlights.
No accessibility regression — text remains legible against its background.

requirements.text: Update the global Tailwind CSS design tokens in frontend/src/index.css and frontend/tailwind.config.js to introduce the new dark tactical palette: add CSS custom properties for deep-sand-dark, aged-bronze, ember-gold, deep-lapis-dark, and command-panel background colors. Ensure these tokens are applied consistently across all game components updated in REQ-14.

quotes: 主体可以参考钢铁命令对画面进行优化

acceptanceCriteria: New CSS custom property tokens for the dark tactical palette are defined in index.css.
tailwind.config.js extends the theme with the new color tokens.
All updated components reference the new tokens rather than hard-coded hex values.
Existing Egyptian gold and turquoise tokens are preserved and supplemented, not removed.

requirements.text: Generate a new dark tactical desert battle board background image to replace or supplement the existing desert background. The image should evoke a night-time aerial view of an Egyptian battlefield with a dark sandy terrain, subtle moonlit dune contours, faint hieroglyph map markings, and a tactical grid overlay texture. Use dimensions 1920×1080px. Also generate an updated dark command panel frame/border image for the game panels, with aged bronze and ember-gold tones, riveted corner details, and worn parchment texture. Use dimensions 960×640px.

quotes: 主体可以参考钢铁命令对画面进行优化

acceptanceCriteria: A new dark battle board background image is saved in frontend/public/assets/generated.
A new dark command panel border/frame image is saved in frontend/public/assets/generated.
The new battle board background is applied to the BattleBoard component.
The new panel frame is applied to EgyptianPanel and all major game panels.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Preserve all existing game logic in useGameState.tsx and combatEngine.ts — only visual/layout changes are in scope.
Egyptian serif font (Cinzel/Cinzel Decorative) must be retained for all heading-level text.
Drag-and-drop unit placement functionality must remain fully operational after the hex grid migration.
nonGoals: Changing the underlying game rules, combat engine logic, or synergy calculations.
Adding new unit types or synergies.
Backend changes — the backend has no game state.
Changing the game title or branding.

required: Aerial night-view Egyptian battlefield with dark sandy terrain, moonlit dune contours, faint hieroglyph map markings, and a tactical grid overlay texture (battle-board-dark-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)
Aged bronze and ember-gold command panel border frame with riveted corner details and worn parchment center texture (command-panel-frame-dark.dim_960x640.png)
